hops.context: PHI-4 14B (Python) outperforms all other models, achieving 48.3% at pass@1 and 63.6% at pass@3.
GEMMA 3 12B follows with 16.9% and 19.6%, respectively, outperforming both GEMMA 2 9B and LLAMA 3.2 3B.
GEMMA 2 9B and LLAMA 3.2 3B, which stay below 11% at pass@3.

question: Which statement correctly reflects the performance hierarchy and type categorization of the models as per the information provided?

options.A: GEMMA 2 9B, a type of algorithm, outperforms PHI-4 14B Python and GEMMA 3 12B in pass@1 and pass@3 metrics.
options.B: Both GEMMA 3 12B and GEMMA 2 9B, as different types of software, outperform PHI-4 14B Python in both pass@1 and pass@3 rates.
options.C: GEMMA 3 12B, categorized as a software, outperforms PHI-4 14B Python in pass@1 rates, but not in pass@3 rates.
options.D: PHI-4 14B Python, being a model, outperforms GEMMA 3 12B, which in turn outperforms GEMMA 2 9B according to their pass@3 rates.

explanation: Option A is correct as it accurately summarizes the performance hierarchy among the models as provided in the context. PHI-4 14B Python is identified as a model that outperforms GEMMA 3 12B, which itself outperforms GEMMA 2 9B, with performance evaluated based on pass@3 rates. Options B, C, and D are incorrect as they misrepresent the performance data and relationships between the models.
context: PHI-4 14B (Python) outperforms all other models, achieving 48.3% at pass@1 and 63.6% at pass@3. | GEMMA 3 12B follows with 16.9% and 19.6%, respectively, outperforming both GEMMA 2 9B and LLAMA 3.2 3B. | GEMMA 2 9B and LLAMA 3.2 3B, which stay below 11% at pass@3.
